title: Beef Tagine with Egyptian Spices
description: A rich and aromatic stew originating from North Africa, made with tender beef and exotic spices.

ingredients.name: Beef (chuck or brisket), cut into 2-inch pieces
ingredients.notes: You can also use beef short ribs or lamb if preferred

ingredients.name: Dense Egyptian spices blend (such as berbere or ras el hanout)
ingredients.notes: You can also use a mix of ground cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and cayenne pepper

ingredients.name: Briny preserved lemon, chopped (optional)
ingredients.notes: You can omit this if you don't have preserved lemon or prefer not to use it

ingredients.name: Salt and black pepper, to taste

instructions: Heat the oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Brown the beef in batches, about 5 minutes per batch, then set aside.
Add more oil if necessary and sauté the onion and garlic until softened.
Add the spices and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the browned beef back into the pot, along with enough water to cover.
Bring to a boil, then cover and transfer to the oven.
Bake at 300°F (150°C) for 2 1/2 hours, or until the beef is tender.
Stir in the preserved lemon (if using) and adjust the seasoning.
Serve hot, garnished with parsley or cilantro.
